top_stories.title: Hackers Use Fake Microsoft Entra Passkey Enrollment to Gain Microsoft 365 Access
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as O-UNC-066 is conducting voice-based phishing attacks that trick Microsoft 365 users into enrolling a fraudulent Microsoft Entra passkey, enabling unauthorized access for data extortion. Okta researchers identified the campaign, which uses a panel-controlled phishing kit specifically designed to subvert the passkey enrollment process across multiple industry sectors.

top_stories.title: 69% of enterprises allow AI agents to share credentials
top_stories.summary: A survey of 107 organizations found that 69% of enterprises allow AI agents to share credentials such as API keys or service-account tokens. This practice creates a security vulnerability where a single compromised agent can grant attackers access to multiple systems and services.

top_stories.title: Extortion crew hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via fake passkey setup
top_stories.summary: A cybercriminal group called "Pink" is conducting social engineering attacks by impersonating IT staff via vishing calls to trick employees into completing fake Microsoft Entra passkey enrollment requests, resulting in account takeovers. The attack chain exploits the passkey setup process within Microsoft 365/Entra ID to gain unauthorized access to corporate accounts.

top_stories.title: Entra passkey enrollment vishing targets Microsoft 365 users
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is conducting vishing (voice phishing) attacks against Microsoft 365 users by impersonating security personnel and requesting victims to enroll a new Microsoft Entra passkey. The campaign targets organizations across multiple sectors, exploiting the passkey enrollment process within Microsoft's Entra identity platform to potentially gain unauthorized access.

top_stories.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of managing AI agents as a distinct identity type within IAM frameworks. It argues that traditional approaches like service accounts or API tokens are insufficient for AI agents, calling for a fundamentally different identity management strategy.

news.title: Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
news.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in the official Gitea Docker image is being actively exploited by hackers. The flaw allows attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators, on self-hosted Gitea instances.

news.title: The Replicant in Your Directory: AI Agents and the Identity Security Gap
news.summary: Netwrix has published analysis on the identity security challenges posed by AI agents and the proliferation of non-human identities in enterprise environments. The piece focuses on visibility gaps and identity governance requirements as AI-driven non-human identities expand the attack surface.

news.title: AWS gives its ERP agent deny-by-default rules and a separate identity
news.summary: AWS has released an ERP automation agent designed with deny-by-default rules and a separate identity for handling accounts receivable tasks. The agent's architecture applies IAM-relevant principles including least-privilege access controls and isolated identity for agentic AI operating within ERP systems.

news.title: Only 28% of financial workforce MFA is phishing-resistant
news.summary: A new Secret Double Octopus report finds that only 28% of MFA used by financial sector workforces is phishing-resistant, with password plus OTP remaining the most common authentication method. The report identifies key challenges preventing broader adoption of phishing-resistant MFA across banking and financial organizations.

news.title: More than half of enterprises report AI agent security incidents, and most are sharing credentials across bots
news.summary: A study found that more than half of enterprises have experienced security incidents involving AI agents, with most organizations sharing credentials across bots. The findings highlight gaps in identity management practices as AI agent deployments scale across enterprise environments.

news.title: Top Privileged Access Management Solutions Named Champions in Info-Tech Research Group's 2026 Data Quadrant Report
news.summary: Info-Tech Research Group's 2026 Privileged Access Management Data Quadrant Report has named Segura, Devolutions PAM, BeyondTrust Platform, and WALLIX PAM as Champions. The rankings are based on verified end-user feedback compiled through the SoftwareReviews platform.

news.title: Barndoor adds SCIM support for Okta, automating user and group provisioning
news.summary: Barndoor has added SCIM support for Okta, enabling automated user and group provisioning within its AI governance platform. The integration allows access to be revoked automatically at offboarding and surfaces policy controls for AI agents and MCP-connected tools.

news.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
news.summary: A cryptomining incident demonstrated how AI gateways can be exploited to gain access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and IAM data. The attack highlights AI gateways as a new attack vector that exposes identity and access management systems to compromise.

news.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
news.summary: A new threat actor group called Helix has emerged, using identity-focused attack techniques including vishing, device code phishing, and MFA abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. The group's methods specifically target authentication mechanisms to gain unauthorized access and exfiltrate data.

news.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
news.summary: GitHub has released npm version 12, which disables install scripts by default and deprecates granular access tokens (GATs) that could bypass two-factor authentication. The changes make previously automatic install behaviors opt-in, reducing supply chain risk and tightening authentication controls in the npm package registry.

news.title: Introducing OAuth Support for AWS MCP Server
news.summary: AWS has introduced OAuth support for its MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server, enabling authentication using the same credentials and sign-in methods used for the AWS Management Console and AWS CLI. The implementation uses a browser-based experience powered by industry-standard OAuth protocols.

news.title: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike, and Cisco pour billions into solving AI’s credential sharing problem
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike, and Cisco are making significant investments targeting AI's credential sharing problem. The investments focus on developing identity solutions to secure AI agent authentication and credential management in enterprise environments.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm has announced an investigation into claims on behalf of SailPoint, Inc. (NASDAQ: SAIL) investors. The notice advises investors to contact the firm but provides no specific details about the nature of the claims being investigated.

news.title: Microsoft launches Windows 365 for Agents to secure AI workloads
news.summary: Microsoft has launched Windows 365 for Agents, a Cloud PC service providing isolated virtual machine environments for running AI agents. The service allows organizations to apply security and compliance frameworks to AI workloads through managed, dedicated virtual machines.

news.title: Common MFA mistakes -- and how to fix them
news.summary: The article examines common mistakes organizations make when implementing and managing MFA that allow attackers to bypass authentication controls. It focuses on improper MFA configuration and management as the primary reasons MFA circumvention attacks succeed.

news.title: Forg365 phishing platform uses AI and device code flows to breach Microsoft 365
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called Forg365 has emerged targeting Microsoft 365 accounts using AI-generated lures and device code flow abuse. The platform exploits OAuth device code authentication flows to bypass MFA and gain unauthorized access to Microsoft 365 accounts.

news.title: FusionAuth Launches Intelligent MFA in Latest Release as Demand Surges for Identity Infrastructure Customers can Control
news.summary: FusionAuth has launched an Intelligent MFA feature in its latest release, built on a 10-signal risk engine that delivers adaptive authentication for customer-controlled deployments. The release coincides with the company reporting 201% quarter-over-quarter bookings growth, indicating strong market demand for its CIAM infrastructure.

news.title: New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called Forg365 has emerged, specifically targeting Microsoft 365 accounts using a combination of adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code authentication attack methods. The platform incorporates AI-assisted lure generation to bypass standard authentication controls and steal credentials.

news.title: 1Kosmos Recognized by Microsoft as Entra Verified ID Partner for Delivering Secure Remote Onboarding and Enhanced Digital Trust
news.summary: 1Kosmos has been recognized by Microsoft as an Entra Verified ID partner for delivering secure remote onboarding using verifiable credentials. The collaboration focuses on reducing hiring fraud, synthetic identities, and unauthorized account creation through integration with Microsoft's Entra Verified ID platform.

news.title: Permiso brings FICO-style risk scores to human, machine and AI identities
news.summary: Permiso Security launched the Risk Score Engine, a model that assigns continuous, multidimensional risk scores to human, machine, and AI identities across organizational environments. The scoring system follows a FICO-style approach, providing unified identity risk assessment across all identity types on its identity security platform.

news.title: ProofID Appoints Neil Evans as Chief AI Officer to Lead Transformation into an AI-Native Identity Security Specialist
news.summary: ProofID, an identity security specialist, has appointed Neil Evans as its newly created Chief AI Officer role. The position is focused on embedding an AI-native mindset across the business, blending commercial, technical, and people leadership.

news.title: Keeper Security surpasses $225M in ARR with transformative growth and is emerging as the market standard for AI-native identity security
news.summary: Keeper Security announced it has surpassed $225 million in annual recurring revenue, positioning itself as a growing player in the identity security market. The company cited product-market fit in agentic AI, identity proliferation, and accelerating growth as drivers toward a stated goal of $1 billion ARR and a potential public offering.

news.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
news.summary: A lone attacker breached an AWS cloud environment within 72 hours by chaining cloud weaknesses with stolen credentials and AI-assisted workflows. The attack culminated in extortion of a large Amazon customer, highlighting credential theft and cloud access vulnerabilities as key attack vectors.

news.title: What's the best way to do authentication in modern applications
news.summary: The article addresses authentication token storage options for modern applications, targeting frontend developers. It covers different approaches to handling login tokens, presenting the tradeoffs between various storage mechanisms.

news.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign is targeting marketing professionals with the goal of stealing Google account credentials. The attack uses nested redirects and other evasion techniques to bypass detection systems.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to a August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and compromise enterprise accounts.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, temporarily seizing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official's profiles. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot into bypassing normal authentication and password reset controls were circulated on Telegram.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers while CISA works to invalidate the leaked credentials.
